equations.raw: oComm(A) = {a in A : [a, o(b)] = 0 for all b in A}
equations.context: Phase 1: Compute o-commutant of the FULL algebra first

equations.raw: That means: the matrix M_j with M_j[k, i] = F^k_{ij} must satisfy M_j @ a = 0.
equations.context: Flatten F_tensor: for each j, the map a -> F(a, e_j) must be zero for all j.

equations.raw: Phase 2: Compute the reverse: {b in A : [a, o(b)] = 0 for all a in A}
equations.context: as Comm(o(A)) intersected with A.

equations.raw: Since o is an anti-automorphism, this means b is in o^{-1}(center) = o(center).
equations.context: Phase 2: Compute the reverse: {b in A : [a, o(b)] = 0 for all a in A} I.e., o(b) commutes with all of A, meaning o(b) is in the center of A.

equations.raw: Start with S0 = full algebra (dim 40)
equations.context: Phase 3: ALTERNATING PROJECTION

equations.raw: Continue until S_n = S_{n+1} (fixed point)
equations.context: S1 = {a in A : [a, o(b)] = 0 for all b in S0} = oComm(S0) = center-like (dim ~5-8) S2 = {a in A : [a, o(b)] = 0 for all b in S1} = larger (since S1 is smaller)

equations.raw: Compute oComm(S_cur) = {a in A : [a, o(b)] = 0 for all b in S_cur}
equations.context: for step in range(20):

equations.raw: Equivalently: sum_i a_i (F^k @ S_cur)_{i, beta} = 0 for all k, beta
equations.context: Compute oComm(S_cur) = {a in A : [a, o(b)] = 0 for all b in S_cur} The constraint is: for all beta, k: sum_i a_i F^k_{i,j} (S_cur)_{j,beta} = 0

equations.raw: F_k = F_tensor[k, :, :]  shape (d_AF, d_AF)
equations.context: constraints = [] for k in range(d_AF):

equations.raw: = sum_i a_i (F_k @ S_cur)[i, beta]... wait, F_tensor[k, i, j] is the (k,i,j) entry
equations.context: F_k = F_tensor[k, :, :]  shape (d_AF, d_AF) F_k @ S_cur: (d_AF, d_S_cur) -- but we want sum_i a_i F_k[i, j] S_cur[j, beta]

equations.raw: = sum_i a_i * (F[k] @ S_cur)[i, beta] = 0
equations.context: = sum_i a_i (F_k @ S_cur)[i, beta]... wait, F_tensor[k, i, j] is the (k,i,j) entry The constraint is: sum_i a_i * sum_j F[k,i,j] * S_cur[j, beta] = 0

equations.raw: Now compute the REVERSE: {b in A : [a, o(b)] = 0 for all a in oComm_S}
equations.context: d_oComm_S = oComm_S.shape[1]

equations.raw: sum_j b_j * sum_i oComm_S[i, alpha] * F[k,i,j] = 0 for all alpha
equations.context: constraints_rev = [] for k in range(d_AF):

equations.raw: = sum_j b_j * (oComm_S^T @ F[k])[:, j] = 0... let me be precise:
equations.context: for k in range(d_AF): sum_j b_j * sum_i oComm_S[i, alpha] * F[k,i,j] = 0 for all alpha

equations.raw: = sum_j b_j (oComm_S[:,alpha]^T @ F[k,:,j]) = 0... no
equations.context: F[k,i,j], alpha indexes oComm_S columns sum_j b_j (sum_i oComm_S[i,alpha] F[k,i,j]) = 0

equations.raw: Let M[alpha,j] = sum_i oComm_S[i,alpha] F[k,i,j]
equations.context: sum_j b_j (sum_i oComm_S[i,alpha] F[k,i,j]) = 0 = sum_j b_j (oComm_S[:,alpha]^T @ F[k,:,j]) = 0... no

equations.raw: = (oComm_S.T @ F[k])[alpha, j]
equations.context: = sum_j b_j (oComm_S[:,alpha]^T @ F[k,:,j]) = 0... no Let M[alpha,j] = sum_i oComm_S[i,alpha] F[k,i,j]
